---
description: "Use when working with ORDA: ds.*, entity selections, queries, entity operations, relations, computed attributes, fromCollection, or datastore classes."
applyTo: '**/*.4dm'
---

# ORDA (Object Relational Data Access)

## Core Types

| Type | Example | Description |
|------|---------|-------------|
| `cs.TableEntity` | `cs.ClientsEntity` | Single record |
| `cs.TableSelection` | `cs.ClientsSelection` | Set of records |
| `ds.Table` | `ds.Clients` | Dataclass (table accessor) |

## Basic Operations

```4d
// Read all / query / get by PK
var $all : cs.ClientsSelection:=ds.Clients.all()
var $active : cs.ClientsSelection:=ds.Clients.query("active = true")
var $one : cs.ClientsEntity:=ds.Clients.get(123)

// Create
var $new : cs.ClientsEntity:=ds.Clients.new()
$new.name:="John"
$new.save()

// Update
var $entity : cs.ClientsEntity:=ds.Clients.get(123)
If ($entity#Null)
    $entity.salary:=$entity.salary*1.1
    $entity.save()
End if

// Delete
$entity.drop()
```

## Query Syntax

### Attribute Names Only

`query()` only accepts **attribute names** — not expressions, concatenations, or computed values. Operators like `+` inside the query string are interpreted as part of the attribute name, causing error 1500.

```4d
// ❌ WRONG — expressions not allowed in query string
ds.Clients.query("firstName+' '+lastName = :1"; $fullName)

// ✅ CORRECT — query individual attributes
ds.Clients.query("firstName = :1 AND lastName = :2"; $first; $last)
```

### Placeholders

```4d
// Positional — :1, :2, :3
ds.Properties.query("region = :1 AND assetType = :2"; $region; $type)

// Same placeholder reused
ds.Clients.query("firstName = :1 OR lastName = :1 OR company = :1"; $search)

// Collection with IN
ds.Properties.query("assetType IN :1"; $typesCollection)

// Wildcard with @
ds.Properties.query("name = :1"; "@"+$search+"@")

// Boolean
ds.Properties.query("needsReembedding = :1"; True)

// Comparison operators
ds.Properties.query("askingYield >= :1 AND askingPrice <= :2"; $minYield; $maxPrice)
```

### Dynamic Query Building

Build query parts as a collection and join with `AND`:

```4d
var $queryParts : Collection:=[]
var $params : Collection:=[]

If ($region#"")
    $queryParts.push("region = :"+String($params.length+1))
    $params.push($region)
End if
If ($minYield>0)
    $queryParts.push("askingYield >= :"+String($params.length+1))
    $params.push($minYield)
End if

var $queryString : Text:=$queryParts.join(" AND ")
// Pass $params via Case of on $params.length, or use named placeholders
```

### Named Placeholders (Preferred for Dynamic Queries)

Use `querySettings.parameters` with named placeholders to avoid `Case of` on param count:

```4d
var $queryString : Text:=""
var $settings : Object:={}
$settings.parameters:={}

If ($region#"")
    $queryString:=$queryString+(($queryString#"") ? " AND " : "")+"region = :region"
    $settings.parameters.region:=$region
End if

var $result : cs.PropertiesSelection:=ds.Properties.query($queryString; $settings)
```

Named params MUST be inside `querySettings.parameters` — passing a flat object does NOT work.

### Null Queries

Placeholders CANNOT be used to search for null. The null value will be evaluated as an error.

```4d
// ❌ WRONG — placeholder with Null does not work
ds.Person.query("spouse = :1"; Null)

// ✅ CORRECT — use literal null keyword
ds.Person.query("spouse = null")
```

### Not-Equal-To Skips Null Values

`#` / `!=` only returns entities where the attribute has a non-null value different from the comparison. Null/undefined values are excluded.

```4d
// Only finds entities where married is explicitly false (not null or missing)
ds.Person.query("info.married # true")

// To include null/undefined:
ds.Person.query("info.married # true OR info.married = null")
```

### Order By in Query String

```4d
// Append "order by" directly to the query string
ds.Properties.query("region = :1 order by name asc"; $region)
ds.Properties.query("active = true order by city asc, name desc")
```

The resulting entity selection is ordered when `order by` is used.

### Formula Queries

Alternative to query strings — useful for complex logic:

```4d
// Formula object
var $formula : Object:=Formula(Length(This.lastName) >= 30)
var $es : cs.EmployeeSelection:=ds.Employee.query($formula)

// Inline eval() in query string
ds.Students.query("eval(length(This.lastname) >= 30) and nationality = :1"; "French")
```

Formulas receive `This` as the current entity. Only `&` and `|` are supported as logical operators inside formulas.

### Collection Attribute Queries

Use `[]` to query inside collection-type object attributes:

```4d
// Find employees with a specific hobby
ds.Employee.query("extra.hobbies[].name = :1"; "tennis")

// Linked arguments — same collection element must match all conditions
ds.Employee.query("extra.hobbies[a].name = :1 AND extra.hobbies[a].level = :2"; "tennis"; 5)
```

Without the `[a]` link, conditions can match different elements.

### Security: Disable Formulas for User Input

When query strings come from user input, disable formula execution:

```4d
var $settings : Object:={}
$settings.allowFormulas:=False
ds.Students.query($userInput; $settings)
```

## Entity Selection Operations

```4d
// Sorting
$es:=ds.Properties.all().orderBy("name asc")
$es:=ds.Properties.all().orderBy("region asc, name desc")

// Extract single attribute as collection
var $names : Collection:=$es.extract("name")

// Distinct values
var $regions : Collection:=ds.Properties.all().distinct("region")

// First / count
var $first : cs.PropertiesEntity:=$es.first()
var $count : Integer:=$es.length

// Convert to collection (for listbox, JSON export)
var $data : Collection:=$es.toCollection("name, region, city, assetType")

// Computed attributes work in toCollection
var $data : Collection:=$es.toCollection("name, displayYield, avatarInitials")

// Bulk delete
ds.MandateFavorites.all().drop()
```

**`.toCollection()` vs `.extract()`**: `.toCollection("attr")` returns `[{"attr": value}, ...]` (objects). `.extract("attr")` returns `[value, ...]` (flat values). For dropdown/popup `values` arrays, always use `.extract()`.

**`EntitySelection[index]` is non-assignable** — you cannot call `.lock()`, `.save()`, or assign attributes directly on it. Assign to a variable first:

```4d
// ❌ WRONG
$sel[0].lastName:="Smith"  // will NOT work
$sel[0].save()             // will NOT work

// ✅ CORRECT
var $entity : cs.EmployeeEntity:=$sel[0]
$entity.lastName:="Smith"
$entity.save()
```

## Entity Class Extensions

Extend entity behavior by creating `TableNameEntity.4dm` classes:

```4d
// File: Sources/Classes/ClientsEntity.4dm
// Computed attributes (read-only)
Function get fullName() : Text
    return This.firstName+" "+This.lastName

Function get avatarInitials() : Text
    return Substring(This.firstName; 1; 1)+Substring(This.lastName; 1; 1)

// Computed via relation traversal
Function get mandateCount() : Integer
    return This.mandates.length
```

- Computed attributes are available in `toCollection()`, queries, and `Form.dataSource` bindings.
- Use `This.relationName` to traverse relations (e.g. `This.mandates`, `This.property`).

## Relation Traversal

Access related entities through defined relation names:

```4d
// N:1 — get parent entity
var $clientName : Text:=$mandate.client.fullName

// 1:N — get child selection
var $mandates : cs.MandatesSelection:=$client.mandates
var $count : Integer:=$client.mandates.length

// Prefer relation traversal over FK queries:
// ❌ WRONG
ds.Mandates.query("clientID = :1"; $client.clientID)
// ✅ CORRECT
$client.mandates
```

## Null Safety

Entity fields return **Null** (not empty string/date/0) when never set. Passing Null to `String()`, using `+` concatenation, or comparing to typed literals causes error 15.

**Rule**: Always check `#Null` before using entity field values in expressions.

```4d
// ❌ WRONG — crashes if field is Null
$label:="Date: "+String($entity.createdAt; ISO date)

// ✅ CORRECT
If ($entity.createdAt#Null)
    $label:="Date: "+String($entity.createdAt; ISO date)
End if

// ✅ Default value pattern
var $model : Text:=Choose($entity.model#Null; $entity.model; "N/A")
```

## Bulk Import with fromCollection

`ds.DataClass.fromCollection()` creates entities in bulk — no manual loop needed.

```4d
var $data : Collection:=JSON Parse(File("/RESOURCES/data.json").getText())
If (ds.Employee.all().length=0)
    ds.Employee.fromCollection($data)
End if
```

- Property names must match dataclass attribute names (unmatched are ignored)
- Text dates in `"YYYY-MM-DD"` are auto-converted to Date fields
- Without a primary key, new entities get auto-generated keys
- Returns an entity selection of created/updated entities

## Save & Error Handling

`.save()` returns a status object — check `success`:

```4d
var $status : Object:=$entity.save()
If (Not($status.success))
    ALERT("Save failed: "+$status.statusText)
End if
```

`.drop()` returns a similar status object.

### Optimistic Locking & Auto Merge

By default, `.save()` uses optimistic locking — it fails if another process modified the entity since it was loaded (stamp changed). Use `dk auto merge` to automatically merge non-conflicting changes:

```4d
var $status : Object:=$entity.save(dk auto merge)
If ($status.success)
    // Saved (possibly merged with concurrent changes)
End if
```

Auto merge fails if both processes changed the **same** attribute.

### Pessimistic Locking

For exclusive access, use `.lock()` / `.unlock()`:

```4d
var $entity : cs.EmployeeEntity:=ds.Employee.get(716)
var $lockStatus : Object:=$entity.lock()
If ($lockStatus.success)
    $entity.salary:=$entity.salary*1.1
    $entity.save()
    $entity.unlock()
Else
    ALERT("Record is locked by another user")
End if
```

- A locked record is automatically unlocked when no references to the entity remain in memory.
- `.unlock()` must be called the same number of times as `.lock()`.

## Vector Similarity Queries (4D 21+)

```4d
// Query by vector similarity
var $results : cs.PropertiesSelection
$results:=ds.Properties.query("vectorDesc > :1"; {vector: $queryVector; metric: mk cosine; threshold: 0.3})

// Sort by similarity (append to query string)
$results:=ds.Properties.query("vectorDesc > :1 order by vectorDesc"; {vector: $queryVector; metric: mk cosine; threshold: 0.3})

// Direct similarity calculation
var $score : Real:=$vectorA.cosineSimilarity($vectorB)
```
